input.content:
# Workflow: Task Management ## Overview A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are no longer needed. ## User Goal The user wants three things in equal measure: 1. **Rapid capture** -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks. 2. **Clear prioritized visibility** -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next. 3. **Completion satisfaction** -- mark things done and watch the list shrink. Success looks like: the user opens the app, immediately sees their prioritized list, can add/edit/reorder/complete/delete tasks with minimal friction, and leaves confident that everything important is tracked. ## Actors - **User** -- A single person managing their own task list. There is no authentication and no support for multiple users. ## Events | # | Event | Description | |---|-------|-------------| | 1 | TaskAdded | A new task was captured. Includes description and a default position at the bottom of the list. | | 2 | TaskCompleted | A task was marked as done. It disappears from the active list. | | 3 | TaskReopened | A completed task was set back to incomplete. It returns to the top of the active list (highest priority). | | 4 | TaskDescriptionChanged | The description text of a task was edited. | | 5 | TaskReprioritized | A task was moved to a new position in the list via drag and drop. | | 6 | TaskDeleted | A task was permanently removed (after user confirmation). | ## Commands | Command | Produces Event | Trigger | Inputs | Rejection Conditions | |---------|---------------|---------|--------|---------------------| | AddTask | TaskAdded | [+ Add] button or Enter key | description | Empty description | | CompleteTask | TaskCompleted | Checkbox on active task | task_id | Already completed | | ReopenTask | TaskReopened | [Reopen] button on completed task | task_id | Not completed | | ChangeTaskDescription | TaskDescriptionChanged | Inline edit confirm (blur/Enter) | task_id, new_description | Empty or unchanged description | | ReprioritizeTask | TaskReprioritized | Drag and drop to new position | task_id, new_position | Same position (no change) | | DeleteTask | TaskDeleted | [x] button + confirmation dialog | task_id | User cancels confirmation | ## Read Models ### ActiveTaskList Powers the main view. Answers: "What are my current tasks, in priority order?" | Field | Source Event(s) | |-------|----------------| | tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | | tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | | tasks[].position | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | | completed_count | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1) | **Filters:** Only tasks where completed = false AND deleted = false. **Ordering:** By position ascending (highest priority first). ### CompletedTaskList Powers the completed tasks view. Answers: "What tasks have I finished?" | Field | Source Event(s) | |-------|----------------| | tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | | tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | **Filters:** Only tasks where completed = true AND deleted = false. **Ordering:** By completion time (most recently completed first), derived from TaskCompleted event timestamp. ## Wireframes ### Active Task List (main view) ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Legend: :: = drag handle (for reordering via drag and drop) [v] = mark complete (checkbox) [x] = delete button [Completed (3)] = link to completed tasks view with count ``` ### Inline Editing ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: [Call the dentist office____] [v] [ x ] | | ^--- inline editing active | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ### Delete Confirmation Dialog ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | Delete Task? | | | | | | | | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | | | permanently removed. | | | | | | | | [Cancel] [Delete] | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ### Completed Tasks View ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | | (No drag handles -- completed tasks have no | | priority order) | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Legend: ~~text~~ = strikethrough (visually indicates completion) [Reopen] = sets task back to incomplete, returns to top of active list [x] = delete button (with confirmation dialog) ``` ### Empty State ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (0)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | No tasks yet. Add one above! | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Automations None. This is a purely user-driven workflow with no automatic reactions. ## Translations (External Integrations) None. The application is entirely self-contained. ## Workflow Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph User["User"] ActiveView["Active Task List
(main view)"] CompletedView["Completed Tasks
(separate view)"] ConfirmDialog["Delete Confirmation
Dialog"] end subgraph Commands["Commands"] AddTask["AddTask"]:::command CompleteTask["CompleteTask"]:::command ReopenTask["ReopenTask"]:::command ChangeDesc["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command Reprioritize["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command DeleteTask["DeleteTask"]:::command end subgraph Events["Events"] TaskAdded["TaskAdded"]:::event TaskCompleted["TaskCompleted"]:::event TaskReopened["TaskReopened"]:::event TaskDescChanged["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event TaskReprioritized["TaskReprioritized"]:::event TaskDeleted["TaskDeleted"]:::event end subgraph Views["Read Models"] ActiveList["ActiveTaskList"]:::view CompletedList["CompletedTaskList"]:::view end ActiveView -->|"type + Add"| AddTask ActiveView -->|"click checkbox"| CompleteTask ActiveView -->|"inline edit"| ChangeDesc ActiveView -->|"drag and drop"| Reprioritize ActiveView -->|"click x"| ConfirmDialog CompletedView -->|"click Reopen"| ReopenTask CompletedView -->|"click x"| ConfirmDialog ConfirmDialog -->|"confirm"| DeleteTask AddTask --> TaskAdded CompleteTask --> TaskCompleted ReopenTask --> TaskReopened ChangeDesc --> TaskDescChanged Reprioritize --> TaskReprioritized DeleteTask --> TaskDeleted TaskAdded --> ActiveList TaskCompleted --> ActiveList TaskCompleted --> CompletedList TaskReopened --> ActiveList TaskReopened --> CompletedList TaskDescChanged --> ActiveList TaskDescChanged --> CompletedList TaskReprioritized --> ActiveList TaskDeleted --> ActiveList TaskDeleted --> CompletedList ActiveList --> ActiveView CompletedList --> CompletedView classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Vertical Slices ### Command Slices 1. [AddTask](slices/add-task.md) -- User adds a new task to the list 2. [CompleteTask](slices/complete-task.md) -- User marks a task as done 3. [ReopenTask](slices/reopen-task.md) -- User sets a completed task back to incomplete 4. [ChangeTaskDescription](slices/change-task-description.md) -- User edits a task's description 5. [ReprioritizeTask](slices/reprioritize-task.md) -- User reorders tasks via drag and drop 6. [DeleteTask](slices/delete-task.md) -- User permanently removes a task ### View Slices 7. [ActiveTaskList](slices/active-task-list.md) -- Read model for the main task list view 8. [CompletedTaskList](slices/completed-task-list.md) -- Read model for the completed tasks view ### Automation Slices None. ### Translation Slices None. ## Business Rules - New tasks are always added to the bottom of the active list (lowest priority). - Reopened tasks are always placed at the top of the active list (highest priority). - Completed tasks are hidden from the active list and visible in a separate completed view. - Deletion requires user confirmation via a dialog. - Deleted tasks cannot be restored from the UI (though events are preserved in the stream). - Tasks can be deleted from either the active list or the completed tasks view. - Task descriptions cannot be empty. ## Key Design Decisions | Decision | Rationale | |----------|-----------| | New tasks go to bottom | Avoids disrupting existing priority order; user can reprioritize afterward | | Reopened tasks go to top | If re-opening, the task is likely urgent or important | | Completed tasks hidden by default | Keeps main list focused on what needs doing | | Separate completed view | Allows review and reopening without cluttering the active list | | Delete confirmation required | Prevents accidental permanent removal | | Delete available in both views | User should not have to navigate between views to clean up | | Inline description editing | Minimal friction; no modal or separate screen needed | | Single list, no categories | Deliberately minimal; priority expressed only through ordering |

input field + Add button"] CMD["AddTask"]:::command EVT["TaskAdded"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"description"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: AddTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | description | text | What needs to be done (non-empty) | **Triggered by:** User types in the input field and clicks [+ Add] or presses Enter. **Rejection conditions:** - Description is empty or blank. ## Event: TaskAdded | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | Unique identifier assigned to the new task | | description | text | The task description provided by the user | | position | number | Position in the list (bottom / lowest priority) | ## Wireframe ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (0)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ Buy groceries_________________] [+ Add] | | ^--- user types here | | | | No tasks yet. # Slice: ReopenTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position). ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Completed Tasks View
Reopen button"] CMD["ReopenTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReopened"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReopenTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The completed task to reopen | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [Reopen] button on a task in the completed tasks view. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not completed (already active). ## Event: TaskReopened | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was reopened | **Business rule:** The reopened task is placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position). This differs from newly added tasks which go to the bottom. Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. ## Wireframe ``` Before (Completed Tasks View): +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [Reopen] on "Send the invoice": +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Active Task List now shows (navigating back): +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Send the invoice [v] [ x ] | | ^--- reopened task at TOP | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task appears at the top (highest priority). Completed count decremented. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed from completed list. ## GWT Scenarios _(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: ChangeTaskDescription ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes the text, and confirms the edit (by pressing Enter or clicking away). The task's description is updated. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
inline edit field"] CMD["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command EVT["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_description"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ChangeTaskDescription | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to edit | | new_description | text | The updated description (non-empty) | **Triggered by:** User clicks on a task description in the active list, edits the text inline, and confirms by pressing Enter or blurring the field. **Rejection conditions:** - New description is empty or blank. - New description is identical to the current description (no change). ## Event: TaskDescriptionChanged | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task whose description changed | | description | text | The new description | ## Wireframe ``` Before (normal display): :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] User clicks on the description text: :: [Call the dentist office____] [v] [ x ] ^--- inline editing active, cursor in text field User presses Enter or clicks away: :: Call the dentist office [v] [ x ] ^--- updated description displayed ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is active). **CompletedTaskList:** Task description updated (if task is completed). ## GWT Scenarios _(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: ReprioritizeTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReprioritizeTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task being moved | | new_position | number | The target position in the list | **Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new position in the active list. **Rejection conditions:** - Task dropped in the same position (no change). ## Event: TaskReprioritized | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | | position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | ## Wireframe ``` Before: :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new position; other tasks shift to accommodate. ## GWT Scenarios _(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: DeleteTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the task is permanently removed from all views. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: DeleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to delete | **Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. **Rejection conditions:** - User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). ## Event: TaskDeleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | **Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. input.content:
# Slice: ActiveTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E5 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: ActiveTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
| tasks[].position | number | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Current priority position |
| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | Add new task at the bottom of the list (highest position number) |
| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
| TaskReopened | Add task back to active list at position 1 (top). Shift existing tasks down. Decrement completed_count. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task order | TaskAdded.position / TaskReprioritized.position / TaskReopened (top) |
| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: CompletedTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: CompletedTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority 7→through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are 8→no longer needed. 9→ 10→## User Goal 11→ 12→The user wants three things in equal measure: 13→ 14→1. **Rapid capture** -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks. 15→2. **Clear prioritized visibility** -- always see an ordered list showing what 16→ to do next. 17→3. **Completion satisfaction** -- mark things done and watch the list shrink. 18→ 19→Success looks like: the user opens the app, immediately sees their prioritized 20→list, can add/edit/reorder/complete/delete tasks with minimal friction, and 21→leaves confident that everything important is tracked. 22→ 23→## Actors 24→ 25→- **User** -- A single person managing their own task list. There is no 26→ authentication and no support for multiple users. 27→ 28→## Events 29→ 30→| # | Event | Description | 31→|---|-------|-------------| 32→| 1 | TaskAdded | A new task was captured. Includes description and a default position at the bottom of the list. | 33→| 2 | TaskCompleted | A task was marked as done. [CompletedTaskList](slices/completed-task-list.md) -- Read model for the completed tasks view 268→ 269→### Automation Slices 270→ 271→None. 272→ 273→### Translation Slices 274→ 275→None. 276→ 277→## Business Rules 278→ 279→- New tasks are always added to the bottom of the active list (lowest priority). 280→- Reopened tasks are always placed at the top of the active list (highest priority). 281→- Completed tasks are hidden from the active list and visible in a separate completed view. 282→- Deletion requires user confirmation via a dialog. 283→- Deleted tasks cannot be restored from the UI (though events are preserved in the stream). 284→- Tasks can be deleted from either the active list or the completed tasks view. 285→- Task descriptions cannot be empty. 286→ 287→## Key Design Decisions 288→ 289→| Decision | Rationale | 290→|----------|-----------| 291→| New tasks go to bottom | Avoids disrupting existing priority order; user can reprioritize afterward | 292→| Reopened tasks go to top | If re-opening, the task is likely urgent or important | 293→| Completed tasks hidden by default | Keeps main list focused on what needs doing | 294→| Separate completed view | Allows review and reopening without cluttering the active list | 295→| Delete confirmation required | Prevents accidental permanent removal | 296→| Delete available in both views | User should not have to navigate between views to clean up | 297→| Inline description editing | Minimal friction; no modal or separate screen needed | 298→| Single list, no categories | Deliberately minimal; priority expressed only through ordering | 299→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. # Workflow: Task Management ## Overview A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are no longer needed. ## User Goal The user wants three things in equal measure: 1. **Rapid capture** -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks. 2. **Clear prioritized visibility** -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next. 3. **Completion satisfaction** -- mark things done and watch the list shrink. Success looks like: the user opens the app, immediately sees their prioritized list, can add/edit/reorder/complete/delete tasks with minimal friction, and leaves confident that everything important is tracked. ## Actors - **User** -- A single person managing their own task list. There is no authentication and no support for multiple users. ## Events | # | Event | Description | |---|-------|-------------| | 1 | TaskAdded | A new task was captured. Includes description and a default position at the bottom of the list. | | 2 | TaskCompleted | A task was marked as done. It disappears from the active list. | | 3 | TaskReopened | A completed task was set back to incomplete. It returns to the top of the active list (highest priority). | | 4 | TaskDescriptionChanged | The description text of a task was edited. | | 5 | TaskReprioritized | A task was moved to a new position in the list via drag and drop. | | 6 | TaskDeleted | A task was permanently removed (after user confirmation). | ## Commands | Command | Produces Event | Trigger | Inputs | Rejection Conditions | |---------|---------------|---------|--------|---------------------| | AddTask | TaskAdded | [+ Add] button or Enter key | description | Empty description | | CompleteTask | TaskCompleted | Checkbox on active task | task_id | Already completed | | ReopenTask | TaskReopened | [Reopen] button on completed task | task_id | Not completed | | ChangeTaskDescription | TaskDescriptionChanged | Inline edit confirm (blur/Enter) | task_id, new_description | Empty or unchanged description | | ReprioritizeTask | TaskReprioritized | Drag and drop to new position | task_id, new_position | Same position (no change) | | DeleteTask | TaskDeleted | [x] button + confirmation dialog | task_id | User cancels confirmation | ## Read Models ### ActiveTaskList Powers the main view. Answers: "What are my current tasks, in priority order?" | Field | Source Event(s) | |-------|----------------| | tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | | tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | | tasks[].position | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | | completed_count | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1) | **Filters:** Only tasks where completed = false AND deleted = false. **Ordering:** By position ascending (highest priority first). ### CompletedTaskList Powers the completed tasks view. Answers: "What tasks have I finished?" | Field | Source Event(s) | |-------|----------------| | tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | | tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | **Filters:** Only tasks where completed = true AND deleted = false. **Ordering:** By completion time (most recently completed first), derived from TaskCompleted event timestamp. ## Wireframes ### Active Task List (main view) ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Legend: :: = drag handle (for reordering via drag and drop) [v] = mark complete (checkbox) [x] = delete button [Completed (3)] = link to completed tasks view with count ``` ### Inline Editing ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: [Call the dentist office____] [v] [ x ] | | ^--- inline editing active | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ### Delete Confirmation Dialog ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | Delete Task? | | | | | | | | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | | | permanently removed. | | | | | | | | [Cancel] [Delete] | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ### Completed Tasks View ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | | (No drag handles -- completed tasks have no | | priority order) | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Legend: ~~text~~ = strikethrough (visually indicates completion) [Reopen] = sets task back to incomplete, returns to top of active list [x] = delete button (with confirmation dialog) ``` ### Empty State ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (0)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | | | | No tasks yet. Add one above! | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Automations None. This is a purely user-driven workflow with no automatic reactions. ## Translations (External Integrations) None. The application is entirely self-contained. ## Workflow Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph User["User"] ActiveView["Active Task List
(main view)"] CompletedView["Completed Tasks
(separate view)"] ConfirmDialog["Delete Confirmation
Dialog"] end subgraph Commands["Commands"] AddTask["AddTask"]:::command CompleteTask["CompleteTask"]:::command ReopenTask["ReopenTask"]:::command ChangeDesc["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command Reprioritize["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command DeleteTask["DeleteTask"]:::command end subgraph Events["Events"] TaskAdded["TaskAdded"]:::event TaskCompleted["TaskCompleted"]:::event TaskReopened["TaskReopened"]:::event TaskDescChanged["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event TaskReprioritized["TaskReprioritized"]:::event TaskDeleted["TaskDeleted"]:::event end subgraph Views["Read Models"] ActiveList["ActiveTaskList"]:::view CompletedList["CompletedTaskList"]:::view end ActiveView -->|"type + Add"| AddTask ActiveView -->|"click checkbox"| CompleteTask ActiveView -->|"inline edit"| ChangeDesc ActiveView -->|"drag and drop"| Reprioritize ActiveView -->|"click x"| ConfirmDialog CompletedView -->|"click Reopen"| ReopenTask CompletedView -->|"click x"| ConfirmDialog ConfirmDialog -->|"confirm"| DeleteTask AddTask --> TaskAdded CompleteTask --> TaskCompleted ReopenTask --> TaskReopened ChangeDesc --> TaskDescChanged Reprioritize --> TaskReprioritized DeleteTask --> TaskDeleted TaskAdded --> ActiveList TaskCompleted --> ActiveList TaskCompleted --> CompletedList TaskReopened --> ActiveList TaskReopened --> CompletedList TaskDescChanged --> ActiveList TaskDescChanged --> CompletedList TaskReprioritized --> ActiveList TaskDeleted --> ActiveList TaskDeleted --> CompletedList ActiveList --> ActiveView CompletedList --> CompletedView classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Vertical Slices ### Command Slices 1. [AddTask](slices/add-task.md) -- User adds a new task to the list 2. [CompleteTask](slices/complete-task.md) -- User marks a task as done 3. [ReopenTask](slices/reopen-task.md) -- User sets a completed task back to incomplete 4. [ChangeTaskDescription](slices/change-task-description.md) -- User edits a task's description 5. [ReprioritizeTask](slices/reprioritize-task.md) -- User reorders tasks via drag and drop 6. [DeleteTask](slices/delete-task.md) -- User permanently removes a task ### View Slices 7. [ActiveTaskList](slices/active-task-list.md) -- Read model for the main task list view 8. [CompletedTaskList](slices/completed-task-list.md) -- Read model for the completed tasks view ### Automation Slices None. ### Translation Slices None. ## Business Rules - New tasks are always added to the bottom of the active list (lowest priority). - Reopened tasks are always placed at the top of the active list (highest priority). - Completed tasks are hidden from the active list and visible in a separate completed view. - Deletion requires user confirmation via a dialog. - Deleted tasks cannot be restored from the UI (though events are preserved in the stream). - Tasks can be deleted from either the active list or the completed tasks view. - Task descriptions cannot be empty. ## Key Design Decisions | Decision | Rationale | |----------|-----------| | New tasks go to bottom | Avoids disrupting existing priority order; user can reprioritize afterward | | Reopened tasks go to top | If re-opening, the task is likely urgent or important | | Completed tasks hidden by default | Keeps main list focused on what needs doing | | Separate completed view | Allows review and reopening without cluttering the active list | | Delete confirmation required | Prevents accidental permanent removal | | Delete available in both views | User should not have to navigate between views to clean up | | Inline description editing | Minimal friction; no modal or separate screen needed | | Single list, no categories | Deliberately minimal; priority expressed only through ordering |

content.content:
1→# Domain Discovery: Todo List
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→The user needs a simple way to manage a prioritized list of next actions. The
     6→application lets them capture tasks, organize them by importance, and track
     7→what has been done -- all in one place with minimal friction.
     8→
     9→This is a demonstration and learning project. The engineering complexity behind
    10→it is intentional; the user-facing experience, however, should feel easy and
    11→smooth.
    12→
    13→## Actors
    14→
    15→- **User** -- A single person managing their own task list. There is no
    16→  authentication and no support for multiple users.
    17→
    18→## User Goals
    19→
    20→- See the current task list immediately upon opening the application.
    21→- Add new tasks to the list.
    22→- Edit the description of an existing task.
    23→- Mark a task as complete.
    24→- Set a completed task back to incomplete.
    25→- Delete a task that is no longer needed.
    26→- Reorder tasks by dragging and dropping them to express priority.
    27→
    28→## Business Concepts
    29→
    30→### Task
    31→
    32→A task is a single item on the user's list. It has three attributes:
    33→
    34→| Attribute        | Description                                                                 |
    35→|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
    36→| Description      | A short piece of text that says what needs to be done.                      |
    37→| Completion state | Whether the task is done or not done.                                       |
    38→| Position         | Where the task sits in the priority-ordered list (higher position = higher priority). |
    39→
    40→Tasks are deliberately minimal. There are no due dates, categories, tags, or
    41→notes.
    42→
    43→### Task List
    44→
    45→The task list is the single, ordered collection of all tasks. There is only one
    46→list per application instance. Priority is expressed entirely through the
    47→ordering of tasks in the list.
    48→
    49→## External Integrations
    50→
    51→None. The application is entirely self-contained with no third-party services.
    52→
    53→## Workflows Identified
    54→
    55→### Task Management
    56→
    57→A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task:
    58→
    59→- **Creation** -- The user adds a new task to the list.
    60→- **Viewing** -- The user sees all tasks and their current state.
    61→- **Editing** -- The user changes a task's description.
    62→- **Completion** -- The user marks a task as done.
    63→- **Reopening** -- The user sets a completed task back to not done.
    64→- **Reordering** -- The user drags a task to a new position to change its priority.
    65→- **Deletion** -- The user removes a task from the list entirely.
    66→
    67→## Suggested Starting Workflow
    68→
    69→**Task Management** is the only workflow, so it is the natural starting point.
    70→Within it, adding a task and viewing the task list should be modeled first,
    71→since every other activity depends on tasks existing and being visible.
    72→

1→# Slice: ReprioritizeTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a 10→new position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks. 11→ 12→## Diagram 13→ 14→```mermaid 15→flowchart LR 16→ UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] 17→ CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command 18→ EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event 19→ VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 20→ 21→ UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD 22→ CMD --> EVT 23→ EVT --> VIEW 24→ 25→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 26→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 27→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 28→``` 29→ 30→## Command: ReprioritizeTask 31→ 32→| Field | Type | Description | 33→|-------|------|-------------| 34→| task_id | identifier | The task being moved | 35→| new_position | number | The target position in the list | 36→ 37→**Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new 38→position in the active list. 39→ 40→**Rejection conditions:** 41→- Task dropped in the same position (no change). 42→ 43→## Event: TaskReprioritized 44→ 45→| Field | Type | Description | 46→|-------|------|-------------| 47→| task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | 48→| position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | 49→ 50→## Wireframe 51→ 52→``` 53→Before: 54→ 55→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 56→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 57→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] 58→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 59→ 60→User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: 61→ 62→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here 63→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 64→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 65→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 66→``` 67→ 68→## Read Model Impact 69→ 70→**ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new 71→position; other tasks shift to accommodate. 72→ 73→## GWT Scenarios 74→ 75→_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_ 76→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Slice: DeleteTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the 10→completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the 11→task is permanently removed from all views. 12→ 13→## Diagram 14→ 15→```mermaid 16→flowchart LR 17→ UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] 18→ UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] 19→ DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] 20→ CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command 21→ EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event 22→ VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 23→ VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view 24→ 25→ UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG 26→ UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG 27→ DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD 28→ CMD --> EVT 29→ EVT --> VIEW1 30→ EVT --> VIEW2 31→ 32→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 33→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 34→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 35→``` 36→ 37→## Command: DeleteTask 38→ 39→| Field | Type | Description | 40→|-------|------|-------------| 41→| task_id | identifier | The task to delete | 42→ 43→**Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then 44→clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. 45→ 46→**Rejection conditions:** 47→- User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). 48→ 49→## Event: TaskDeleted 50→ 51→| Field | Type | Description | 52→|-------|------|-------------| 53→| task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | 54→ 55→**Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. The event 56→stream retains the full history, but the UI provides no way to restore a 57→deleted task. 58→ 59→## Wireframe 60→ 61→``` 62→User clicks [x] on "Fix the leaky faucet": 63→ 64→+--------------------------------------------------+ 65→| | 66→| +--------------------------------------------+ | 67→| | Delete Task? | | 68→| | | | 69→| | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | 70→| | permanently removed. | | 71→| | | | 72→| | [Cancel] [Delete] | | 73→| +--------------------------------------------+ | 74→| | 75→+--------------------------------------------------+ 76→ 77→If user clicks [Delete], the task disappears from the list. 78→If user clicks [Cancel], nothing changes. 79→``` 80→ 81→## Read Model Impact 82→ 83→**ActiveTaskList:** Task removed (if it was active). content.content:
1→# Slice: ActiveTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
    10→(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.
    11→
    12→## Diagram
    13→
    14→```mermaid
    15→flowchart LR
    16→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    17→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    18→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    19→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    20→    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E5 --> VIEW
    29→    E6 --> VIEW
    30→
    31→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    32→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    33→```
    34→
    35→## Read Model: ActiveTaskList
    36→
    37→### Fields
    38→
    39→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    40→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    41→| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
    42→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    43→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    44→| tasks[].position | number | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Current priority position |
    45→| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |
    46→
    47→### Filters
    48→
    49→- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.
    50→
    51→### Ordering
    52→
    53→- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).
    54→
    55→### Event Processing Rules
    56→
    57→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    58→|-------|---------------------|
    59→| TaskAdded | Add new task at the bottom of the list (highest position number) |
    60→| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
    61→| TaskReopened | Add task back to active list at position 1 (top). Shift existing tasks down. Decrement completed_count. |
    62→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
    63→| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
    64→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |
    65→
    66→### Information Completeness Check
    67→
    68→| Display Element | Source |
    69→|----------------|--------|
    70→| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    71→| Task order | TaskAdded.position / TaskReprioritized.position / TaskReopened (top) |
    72→| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
    73→| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    74→
    75→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    76→
    77→## Wireframe
    78→
    79→```
    80→+--------------------------------------------------+
    81→|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
    82→+--------------------------------------------------+
    83→|                                                    |
    84→|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
    85→|                                                    |
    86→|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
    87→|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
    88→|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
    89→|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
    90→|                                                    |
    91→+--------------------------------------------------+
    92→```
    93→
    94→## GWT Scenarios
    95→
    96→_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_
    97→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→# Slice: CompletedTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
    10→tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
    11→and reopen or delete tasks.
    12→
    13→## Diagram
    14→
    15→```mermaid
    16→flowchart LR
    17→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    18→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    19→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    20→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E6 --> VIEW
    29→
    30→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    31→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Read Model: CompletedTaskList
    35→
    36→### Fields
    37→
    38→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    39→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    40→| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
    41→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    42→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    43→
    44→### Filters
    45→
    46→- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.
    47→
    48→### Ordering
    49→
    50→- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
    51→  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.
    52→
    53→### Event Processing Rules
    54→
    55→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    56→|-------|---------------------|
    57→| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
    58→| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
    59→| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
    60→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
    61→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |
    62→
    63→### Information Completeness Check
    64→
    65→| Display Element | Source |
    66→|----------------|--------|
    67→| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    68→| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
    69→| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    70→
    71→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    72→
    73→## Wireframe

    75→```
    76→+--------------------------------------------------+
    77→|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |

    79→|                                                    |

    81→|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    82→|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    83→|                                                    |
    84→+--------------------------------------------------+
    85→```

content.text:
I have now read all 10 files. Let me perform the completeness check systematically.

---

## Information Completeness Check: Task Management Workflow

### 1. Read Model to Event Traceability

**ActiveTaskList:**

| Field | Source Event(s) | Status |
|-------|----------------|--------|
| tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | Traceable |
| tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Traceable |
| tasks[].position | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Traceable |
| completed_count | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Traceable |

**CompletedTaskList:**

| Field | Source Event(s) | Status |
|-------|----------------|--------|
| tasks[].task_id | TaskAdded.task_id | Traceable |
| tasks[].description | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Traceable |
| ordering (completion time) | TaskCompleted event timestamp | Traceable |

All read model fields trace to events. No gaps.

### 2. Event Naming

| Event | Past Tense? | Business Language? | Status |
|-------|------------|-------------------|--------|
| TaskAdded | Yes | Yes | Pass |
| TaskCompleted | Yes | Yes | Pass |
| TaskReopened | Yes | Yes | Pass |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Yes | Yes | Pass |
| TaskReprioritized | Yes | Yes | Pass |
| TaskDeleted | Yes | Yes | Pass |

All 6 events are past tense and use business language. No issues.

### 3. Command Coverage (Event to Trigger)

| Event | Trigger | Status |
|-------|---------|--------|
| TaskAdded | AddTask command (user clicks Add or presses Enter) | Covered |
| TaskCompleted | CompleteTask command (user clicks checkbox) | Covered |
| TaskReopened | ReopenTask command (user clicks Reopen) | Covered |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | ChangeTaskDescription command (user edits inline) | Covered |
| TaskReprioritized | ReprioritizeTask command (user drags and drops) | Covered |
| TaskDeleted | DeleteTask command (user confirms deletion) | Covered |

All 6 events have triggering commands. No gaps.

### 4. Command Validation Rules

| Command | Rejection Conditions | Status |
|---------|---------------------|--------|
| AddTask | Empty description | Defined |
| CompleteTask | Already completed | Defined |
| ReopenTask | Not completed (already active) | Defined |
| ChangeTaskDescription | Empty/blank description; unchanged description | Defined |
| ReprioritizeTask | Same position (no change) | Defined |
| DeleteTask | User cancels confirmation (command never issued) | Defined |

All commands have rejection conditions. No gaps.

### 5. Read Model Coverage (Actor Information Needs)

The single actor (User) needs:
- See current tasks in priority order -- covered by **ActiveTaskList**
- See completed tasks -- covered by **CompletedTaskList**
- Know how many tasks are completed (badge) -- covered by **ActiveTaskList.completed_count**
- Empty state messaging -- covered (derived from empty task list)
- Delete confirmation dialog (needs task description to display) -- covered by task description in both read models

All actor information needs are covered. No gaps.

### 6. Automation Analysis

The workflow declares "None" for automations. This is correct: all 6 events are triggered exclusively by user commands. There are no event-to-event chains, no automated reactions, and therefore no loop risks.

No issues.

### 7. Translation Coverage

The workflow declares "None" for external integrations. The domain overview confirms "None. The application is entirely self-contained with no third-party services." This is consistent.

No issues.

### 8. Cross-Slice Consistency

**Event field consistency across slices and overview:**

| Element | Workflow Overview | Slice Definition | Consistent? |
|---------|------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| TaskAdded fields (task_id, description, position) | Described in overview events table | Matches add-task.md event table | Yes |
| TaskCompleted fields (task_id) | Described in overview | Matches complete-task.md | Yes |
| TaskReopened fields (task_id) | Described in overview | Matches reopen-task.md | Yes |
| TaskDescriptionChanged fields (task_id, description) | Described in overview | Matches change-task-description.md | Yes |
| TaskReprioritized fields (task_id, position) | Described in overview | Matches reprioritize-task.md | Yes |
| TaskDeleted fields (task_id) | Described in overview | Matches delete-task.md | Yes |

**Read model references across slices:**

Each command slice correctly identifies which read models are affected:
- AddTask: ActiveTaskList -- correct
- CompleteTask: ActiveTaskList + CompletedTaskList -- correct
- ReopenTask: ActiveTaskList + CompletedTaskList -- correct
- ChangeTaskDescription: ActiveTaskList + CompletedTaskList -- correct
- ReprioritizeTask: ActiveTaskList only -- correct (completed tasks have no position)
- DeleteTask: ActiveTaskList + CompletedTaskList -- correct

**Read model slice event processing matches command slices:**

The ActiveTaskList slice lists processing rules for all 6 events. The CompletedTaskList slice lists processing rules for 5 events (excludes TaskReprioritized, which is correct since completed tasks have no position ordering). Both are internally consistent.

No cross-slice inconsistencies found.

### 9. Slice Self-Containment

Each of the 8 slices includes:
- Pattern identification (command or view)
- Summary
- Mermaid diagram
- Field definitions with types
- Trigger mechanism
- Rejection conditions (for commands)
- Event processing rules (for views)
- Wireframe showing before/after state
- Read model impact section
- GWT scenarios placeholder

All slices are self-contained with sufficient detail to implement.

### 10. Edge Case Review

I examined the following edge cases and verified they are handled:

- **Adding a task to an empty list:** Covered by empty state wireframe in add-task.md and active-task-list.md
- **Completing the last active task:** ActiveTaskList would show empty state; completed_count would be 1
- **Reopening when active list is empty:** TaskReopened places task at top (position 1), which works fine
- **Deleting a completed task:** DeleteTask slice explicitly covers both active and completed views; ActiveTaskList event processing correctly decrements completed_count when a completed task is deleted
- **Editing description of a completed task:** ChangeTaskDescription affects CompletedTaskList -- however, the wireframe and trigger description in the slice only mention inline editing from the active list view. The completed tasks view wireframe shows no inline edit mechanism.

This last point is the only item I want to raise.

---

## Result

The event model is remarkably thorough and consistent. I found one minor ambiguity worth clarifying:

**Potential Gap: Can completed tasks have their descriptions edited?**

The `ChangeTaskDescription` slice says:
- **Triggered by:** "User clicks on a task description in the active list"
- The diagram source UI is labeled "Active Task List / inline edit field"
- The wireframe only shows inline editing in the active task list

Yet the read model impact says: "CompletedTaskList: Task description updated (if task is completed)." And the CompletedTaskList read model event processing rules include TaskDescriptionChanged.

This is not necessarily a contradiction -- the read model correctly handles the event regardless of where it was triggered, and a completed task's description could have been changed while it was still active. The CompletedTaskList projection just needs to reflect whatever the latest description is.

However, looking at the completed tasks wireframe, there is no inline edit affordance (no click-to-edit on the strikethrough text). The completed view only shows [Reopen] and [x] buttons. This means the only way a completed task's description gets updated in the CompletedTaskList is if it was changed via a *prior* TaskDescriptionChanged event (before completion). The model handles this correctly through event replay.

**Verdict:** This is consistent as designed. The ChangeTaskDescription command is only available from the active list, and the CompletedTaskList projection correctly reflects historical description changes. No actual gap.

---

## Completeness Check: PASSED

  Missing rules: None

Read Model Coverage:
  Actors: User (single actor)
  Information needs covered: Yes (active list, completed list, completed count, empty state, delete confirmation)

Automation Analysis:

Translation Coverage:
  External integrations: None
  ACL coverage: N/A

  Event processing rules: Complete and non-contradictory

  All 8 slices: Pattern, summary, diagram, fields, triggers, wireframes, read model impact

RESULT: No gaps found. All read model fields trace to events. All events have
triggers. All commands have validation rules. No automations to check. No
external integrations. Cross-slice references are consistent.

Ready to proceed.

# Slice: ReopenTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position). ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Completed Tasks View
Reopen button"] CMD["ReopenTask"]:::command EVT["TaskReopened"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: ReopenTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The completed task to reopen | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [Reopen] button on a task in the completed tasks view. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not completed (already active). ## Event: TaskReopened | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was reopened | **Business rule:** The reopened task is placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position). This differs from newly added tasks which go to the bottom. Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. ## Wireframe ``` Before (Completed Tasks View): +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Send the invoice~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [Reopen] on "Send the invoice": +--------------------------------------------------+ | [< Back to Tasks] Completed Tasks | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | ~~Order new lightbulbs~~ [Reopen] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Active Task List now shows (navigating back): +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Send the invoice [v] [ x ] | | ^--- reopened task at TOP | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task appears at the top (highest priority). Completed count decremented. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed from completed list. ## GWT Scenarios _(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: DeleteTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the task is permanently removed from all views. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff ``` ## Command: DeleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to delete | **Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. **Rejection conditions:** - User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). ## Event: TaskDeleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | **Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. The event stream retains the full history, but the UI provides no way to restore a deleted task. ## Wireframe ``` User clicks [x] on "Fix the leaky faucet": +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | Delete Task? | | | | | | | | "Fix the leaky faucet" will be | | | | permanently removed. | | | | | | | | [Cancel] [Delete] | | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ If user clicks [Delete], the task disappears from the list. If user clicks [Cancel], nothing changes. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed (if it was active). Remaining tasks may shift positions. **CompletedTaskList:** Task removed (if it was completed). ## GWT Scenarios _(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: ActiveTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E5 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: ActiveTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
| tasks[].position | number | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Current priority position |
| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | Add new task at the bottom of the list (highest position number) |
| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
| TaskReopened | Add task back to active list at position 1 (top). Shift existing tasks down. Decrement completed_count. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task order | TaskAdded.position / TaskReprioritized.position / TaskReopened (top) |
| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

# Slice: CompletedTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: CompletedTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_

content.text: Good, I have the full picture. Now let me unleash the GWT agent upon these slices. Given the volume — 6 command slices and 2 view slices — I'll send it off with everything it needs to work through them all. It may have questions for you along the way.

input.prompt:
You are generating GWT (Given-When-Then) scenarios for all 8 slices of the Task Management workflow in a Todo List application.

**Project path:** /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list
**Branch:** event-model/task-management
**Workflow:** docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/

**IMPORTANT: Ask the user ONE question at a time if you need clarification. Use the user-input-protocol: write your question to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jwilger-projects-todo-list/d9d5490d-b136-4afa-b591-23808bde2eba/scratchpad/user-input-request.md and then STOP and return, saying you need user input.**

**GWT Pattern Rules:**

For **Command slices** (AddTask, CompleteTask, ReopenTask, ChangeTaskDescription, ReprioritizeTask, DeleteTask):
- Given = prior events that set up state
- When = the command is issued
- Then = events produced OR error/rejection

For **View slices** (ActiveTaskList, CompletedTaskList):
- Given = current projection state
- When = a new event arrives
- Then = resulting projection state

**Use concrete example data** — real task descriptions, real positions, real counts. Not abstract placeholders.

**For each slice, write:**
1. Happy path scenario(s)
2. Edge cases and error scenarios
3. Use real example data (e.g., "Buy groceries", "Call the dentist")

**Here is the complete context for each slice:**

---

### SLICE 1: AddTask (Command)
- Command: AddTask with description (text)
- Event: TaskAdded with task_id (identifier), description (text), position (number)
- New tasks go to BOTTOM of list (lowest priority)
- Rejection: empty description
- System assigns task_id and position

### SLICE 2: CompleteTask (Command)
- Command: CompleteTask with task_id
- Event: TaskCompleted with task_id
- Task disappears from active list, appears in completed view
- Rejection: task already completed

### SLICE 3: ReopenTask (Command)
- Command: ReopenTask with task_id
- Event: TaskReopened with task_id
- Reopened task goes to TOP of active list (highest priority)
- Rejection: task is not completed (already active)

### SLICE 4: ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
- Command: ChangeTaskDescription with task_id, new_description
- Event: TaskDescriptionChanged with task_id, description
- Inline editing in active task list
- Rejection: empty/blank description, or description unchanged

### SLICE 5: ReprioritizeTask (Command)
- Command: ReprioritizeTask with task_id, new_position
- Event: TaskReprioritized with task_id, position
- Drag and drop reordering
- Rejection: same position (no change)

### SLICE 6: DeleteTask (Command)
- Command: DeleteTask with task_id
- Event: TaskDeleted with task_id
- Requires confirmation dialog
- Can delete from active list OR completed tasks view
- Permanent from UI perspective (no undo)

### SLICE 7: ActiveTaskList (View)
- Shows incomplete, non-deleted tasks ordered by position
- Fields: tasks[].task_id, tasks[].description, tasks[].position, completed_count
- Processes all 6 events
- Position 1 = highest priority (top)

### SLICE 8: CompletedTaskList (View)
- Shows completed, non-deleted tasks ordered by completion time (most recent first)
- Fields: tasks[].task_id, tasks[].description
- Processes 5 events (not TaskReprioritized)

---

**Business rules to encode in scenarios:**
- New tasks always go to bottom
- Reopened tasks always go to top
- Completed tasks hidden from active list
- Delete available from both views
- Description cannot be empty
- Single user, single list, no auth
- Deliberately minimal: no due dates, categories, tags

**Output:** For each slice file, REPLACE the placeholder `_(To be generated by sdlc:gwt agent)_` with the actual GWT scenarios. Keep the existing content in each file intact — only replace the GWT placeholder section.

The slice files are at:
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/add-task.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/complete-task.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reopen-task.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/change-task-description.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reprioritize-task.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/delete-task.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/active-task-list.md
- docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/completed-task-list.md

Write thorough but concise scenarios. Use the GWT format consistently. Write all 8 files without stopping unless you genuinely need user clarification on a business rule.
